text.title: WebApplicationFactory Integration Tests
text.summary: Integration test patterns using WebApplicationFactory for real HTTP semantics without a listening socket.

rule.statement: Inherit from IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>> for expensive shared setup. Add public partial class Program {} in Program.cs for .NET 6+ minimal hosting visibility.
rule.rationale: IClassFixture shares one factory instance per test class, avoiding repeated startup cost while keeping class-level isolation.

rule.statement: Tag database-touching tests with [Collection("DB")] or DisableParallelization=true. Use Respawn to reset DB state between tests in the same fixture.
rule.rationale: xUnit runs tests in parallel within a collection by default; database-touching tests without isolation produce cross-test contamination and flaky failures.

rule.statement: Tag slow integration tests with [Trait("Category", "Integration")] and run them in a separate CI stage.
rule.rationale: Mixing unit and integration tests in one dotnet test run serializes CI and inflates feedback loop to engineers.

rule.statement: Use Testcontainers (PostgreSql, Redis) for integration tests requiring real infrastructure. Implement IAsyncLifetime on the fixture for container lifecycle management.
rule.rationale: Missing IAsyncLifetime causes container leaks across test runs; Testcontainers without it produce orphaned Docker containers.

text.reference: WebApplicationFactory integration test skeleton with database reset.
Agent entry point: dotnet test + coverlet + line coverage threshold.
